Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Duke Bakery Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Duke Bakery Pte Ltd was in the business of producing and selling confectionery and bakery products
Duke Bakery operated a central kitchen and had 12 retail outlets as of August 2024
Mr Lin loaned $150,000 to Duke Bakery in January 2017 to help pay a deposit for a new store in Bedok
Mr Cai communicated with Mr Zhang about Mr Lin's $150,000 loan to Duke Bakery
A Letter of Agreement was signed requiring Duke Bakery to repay Mr Lin's loan by 10 February 2017
Mr Zhang described Mr Lin's loan as temporary and requiring repayment

Party_Details.Rule: A company is bound by the terms of a loan agreement unless a valid and mutually agreed modification exists
Party_Details.Application: The loan was subject to a Transfer Agreement, the loan was temporary and repayable
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found no evidence of the Transfer Agreement and upheld the validity of the Letter of Agreement, requiring repayment of the loan.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Lin Liming

Facts.Text: Lin Liming was a former director of Duke Bakery
Lin Liming loaned $150,000 to Duke Bakery
Lin Liming discussed the loan with Mr Cai
A Letter of Agreement was signed for the repayment of the loan by 10 February 2017
Lin Liming received repayments for the loan from Duke Bakery

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Lin Liming was legally responsible for receiving loan repayments under a disputed Transfer Agreement
Party_Details.Rule: A lender is entitled to repayment unless a valid agreement to the contrary is proven
Party_Details.Application: It was subject to a Transfer Agreement preventing repayment
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found no evidence of the Transfer Agreement and upheld the validity of the Letter of Agreement, allowing repayment.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Zhang Yongqiang

Facts.Text: Zhang Yongqiang was a former Managing Director of Duke Bakery
Zhang Yongqiang communicated with Mr Cai about the repayment of Mr Lin's loan
Zhang Yongqiang described Mr Lin's loan as temporary and requiring repayment
Zhang Yongqiang signed a Letter of Agreement for the repayment of Mr Lin's loan

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Zhang Yongqiang was legally responsible for authorizing loan repayments under a disputed Transfer Agreement
Party_Details.Rule: A director must act within the scope of authority and in accordance with valid agreements
Party_Details.Application: The loan was subject to a Transfer Agreement preventing repayment
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found no evidence of the Transfer Agreement and upheld the validity of the Letter of Agreement, allowing repayment.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Chng Chee Hong

Facts.Text: Chng Chee Hong was Duke Bakery's former Finance Manager
Chng Chee Hong misappropriated a significant sum from Duke Bakery while she was Finance Manager
Chng Chee Hong told Mr Cai in a WeChat conversation that, based on her own calculations, she had taken about $380,000
Chng Chee Hong repaid $420,955 to Duke Bakery

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a finance manager is legally responsible for specific unrecovered financial shortfalls discovered during a corporate audit
Party_Details.Rule: An employee tasked with financial control must account for all missing corporate funds and is legally responsible for any unauthorized diversions
Party_Details.Application: The finance manager remained responsible for further unrecovered sums beyond her initial repayments. she had already repaid all relevant amounts and was not responsible for the remaining unaccounted discrepancies
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that while the finance manager had taken funds, she had already repaid the $420,955, and the company failed to provide sufficient evidence linking her to the remaining unrecovered sums. The main claim against her was dismissed.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Chng Chee Hong

Facts.Text: Chng Chee Hong was a former Finance Manager of Duke Bakery
Chng Chee Hong misappropriated funds from Duke Bakery over the period 2015 to 2018
Chng Chee Hong communicated with Mr Cai about the amount misappropriated
Chng Chee Hong repaid $420,955 to Duke Bakery

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Chng Chee Hong is entitled to recover the excess sum repaid to Duke Bakery
Party_Details.Rule: A party may recover payments made under a mistake or without a valid basis
Party_Details.Application: The repayment was voluntary and not subject to recovery
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found no evidence of a promise by Duke Bakery not to pursue legal action and dismissed the claim for recovery of the excess sum.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Duke Bakery Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Duke Bakery Pte Ltd was in the business of producing and selling confectionery and bakery products
Duke Bakery received $420,955 in repayments from Chng Chee Hong

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Duke Bakery is legally responsible to return the excess sum repaid by Chng Chee Hong
Party_Details.Rule: A recipient of funds is not required to return them unless a valid claim of mistake or lack of basis is proven
Party_Details.Application: The repayment was made under a mistaken belief of obligation
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found no evidence of a promise by Duke Bakery not to pursue legal action and dismissed the claim for recovery of the excess sum.
